summary: This cluster examines the role of ship ballast water as a vector for the global dispersal of harmful algal bloom (HAB) species, with a specific focus on dinoflagellate cysts. Research conducted in Tampa Bay, Florida, USA, recovered 1,633 dinoflagellate cysts and cyst-like cells from ballast water and sediment samples, demonstrating that ships exchanging ballast water in this region can transport HAB species to other ports. The findings highlight a significant risk of cross-contamination and the potential expansion of existing blooms into new geographic areas, contributing to a broader understanding of how maritime traffic impacts marine ecosystem health.
